tranche: UP11-2 local env injection + health test (bounded)
purpose: Inject available local RunPod envs from root .env into the live local k3d AMOF controlplane and report the truthful heavy-lane health outcome. Do not claim usability beyond what /api/v1/runpod/heavy-lane/health proves.

cp1_env_census.verdict: only RUNPOD_API_KEY is available locally; heavy lane cannot be fully usable without an OpenAI-compatible endpoint URL, which requires a real T1 pod proof (out of scope this slice)

infrastructure_side_fix.reason: After RUNPOD_API_KEY was set, the /heavy-lane/health probe started calling the live https://rest.runpod.io/v1 REST API and returned HTTP 500 because the k3d cluster CoreDNS upstream (172.18.0.1 -> docker embedded DNS) returned SERVFAIL for external names. Controlplane pod had no IPv4 DNS resolution for external hosts despite full TCP/443 connectivity.
infrastructure_side_fix.scope: controlplane deployment only; other AMOF workloads and the cluster-wide CoreDNS config untouched
infrastructure_side_fix.bounded_rationale: fixes the pod-local DNS symptom without broadening to cluster infra or code changes

workloads_restarted: deploy/amof-amof-control-plane-controlplane (one rollout for env, one for dnsConfig, plus one for fresh-key resync)
cp2_local_injection.cluster_wide_changes: none (CoreDNS restart was a diagnostic; the cluster-wide Corefile is unchanged)

cp3_health_proof.truthful_classification: still unusable because RUNPOD_OPENAI_BASE_URL is not configured and no serverless/pod-based RunPod endpoint exists for the account (endpoint_count=0). RUNPOD_API_KEY is now correctly wired (api_key_configured=true, template_count=1), proving the credential flows end-to-end.

cp4_operator_smoke.note: UI verification is API-level (the /runpod page calls these same endpoints via useRunpodHeavyLaneHealth hook). A human browser login would display exactly the missing_prerequisites list above.

cp5_closure.code_or_config_file_change: none (runtime-only: kubectl set env + kubectl patch dnsConfig)
cp5_closure.single_real_blocker: RUNPOD_OPENAI_BASE_URL is unset in .env because no pod-based or serverless RunPod OpenAI-compatible endpoint has been proven yet (T1 is still operator-gated)

next_slice_name: UP11-3 T1 live pod-based endpoint proof (unchanged from UP11-1 closeout)
next_slice_scope_hint: Run repos/amof/scripts/runpod_t1_drive.py --profile gpt-oss-120b --max-wait-minutes 45 --stop-on-success --audit-path ecosystems/amof-platform/audit/$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)-runpod-t1-pod-endpoint-proof.json (after copying the example profile into .amof/runpod-profiles/)

On usable verdict, paste the proven URL as RUNPOD_OPENAI_BASE_URL= into .env, re-run kubectl set env against the local controlplane (same tool we used here), and re-probe /api/v1/runpod/heavy-lane/health to confirm status=usable with model_count>=1
mutations_performed: deploy rollouts as a consequence of the two patches
